threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You pause at a crossing for more than three seconds, thinking you have the right of way. Locals are already halfway down the street.
Caught a late 59 in Brixton and overheard two mates arguing about who snored loudest on the last bus home. Classic night bus banter!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was really living here? Hearing an opera performance wafting out of a window while a dog barked along on the street.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you sip coffee while a giant bear mural silently watches the morning hustle; it’s like having a guardian for your caffeine fix.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they complain about the bus delays while casually dodging the dog on the tracks at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center just as the dog jumped off the tracks—nothing like a canine interruption during rush hour to capture SF's charm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Gràcia are for their Instagram shots, but locals dodge the splashes while enjoying a quiet beer nearby.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their endless selfies in front of Park Güell—locals just go for a stroll and avoid that selfie spot altogether.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the bus splashes you as it whizzes by. Proper Hackney locals know to stand back, mate.
Heard a guy on the 133 last night promise to his mate he'd never ride a night bus again after that rat ran over his shoe. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a girl in a dirndl ordering falafel at 2 AM while the kebab shop blasted techno. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand with a rotating DJ set. It's the perfect mix of street food and Berlin nightlife, truly unique.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can name five bars on Valencia and still complain about the taco truck lines being too long.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 this morning, and the driver yelled 'next stop, the Land of Misfit Toys!' as someone hopped on with a parrot on their shoulder.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘tapas’ means small plates everywhere; locals know you ask for ‘pintxos’ at the bar next door for the good stuff.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment at the tram schedules—often staring at the signs like they’re hieroglyphs. Classic sign of a newcomer.
